title: lophophora williamsii subsp. diffusa
intro: Lophophora williamsii subsp. diffusa, commonly known as the 'Mazapiltepec peyote', is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Lophophora williamsii cactus, native to northern Mexico. This plant is a succulent species that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its rich content of psychoactive compounds, particularly mescaline. The medicinal properties of Lophophora williamsii subsp. diffusa include its ability to induce a sense of calm and relaxation, as well as its potential in treating pain, anxiety, and insomnia. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made through drying and grinding the cactus pads, which can be consumed in small amounts under the guidance of a medical professional, and are said to offer various health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved mood regulation.
